title: Monsoon Alert – Heavy Rain Warning till March 12, Thunderstorms Expected in These States - Times Bull
article_text:
New Delhi: The weather is rapidly changing these days, causing temperatures to rise in some areas and fall due to snowfall in others. Snowfall continues to cause temperatures to drop below freezing in many areas, making life difficult for people. Some states across the country are currently experiencing rain and thunderstorms, causing temperatures to drop.
Fog enveloped some areas of Uttar Pradesh early this morning, causing inconvenience to residents. The weather will disrupt normal life until March 12th. The Indian Meteorological Department has issued a warning for heavy rain in several states across the country. Details about the weather conditions can be found below.
According to the Meteorological Department, the weather in Kerala, South India, is also not going to be pleasant. Rain continues here. The weather in Kerala may now play a new game. Consequently, the Meteorological Department has issued an alert that heavy rains are likely to occur in Kerala on March 9th, 10th, 11th, and 12th.
Himachal Pradesh received a good amount of rainfall this monsoon season. The rains haven’t completely stopped yet. Now, the weather in Himachal Pradesh may take on a new colour. The International Monetary FundIMFMD) has issued an alert that Kerala may experience heavy rains on March 9, 10, 11, and 12.
The IMD has issued a heavy rain alert for Uttarakhand, Bihar, Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Jammu and Kashmir. In addition, a heavy rain warning has been issued for Ladakh, Mahe, Lakshadweep, Andaman and Nicobar, Puducherry, Yanam, and Rayalaseema until March 12.
Rain is also expected in West Bengal, Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh, Odisha, Manipur, and Mizoram. Heavy rain is also expected in Meghalaya, Nagaland, and Tripura on March 9, 10, 11, and 12.
Winter is slowly withdrawing. Temperatures have risen in Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and Jharkhand. The afternoon sun is making people sweat. This means the temperature is gradually approaching zero. In Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, and Jammu and Kashmir, the cold continues amid snowfall.
